items.blurb: Spurs offensive avalanche buries Timberwolves in Game 6 — The San Antonio Spurs are known for their lockdown defense. In Game 6 of their second-round series, they overwhelmed the Minnesota Timberwolves with an offensive barrage.

items.headline: Kings May Pursue Mitchell Robinson In Free Agency
items.blurb: Kings May Pursue Mitchell Robinson In Free Agency — The New York Knicks hope to re-sign Mitchell Robinson, who is entering unrestricted free agency. However, the Knicks ever-increasing payroll could force the team into making a touch decision on Robinson. If New York waffles on re-signing Robinson, the Sacramento Kings could be poised to pounce. items.blurb: NBA Sets MVP Announcement For Sunday, One Day Before Thunder Host Spurs In West Finals — The NBA will announce its Most Valuable Player award Sunday evening, one day before Game 1 of the Western Conference finals between the Oklahoma City Thunder and San Antonio Spurs. items.blurb: Spurs Backcourt Trio Of Castle, Fox, Harper Powers 139-109 Game 6 Elimination Of Wolves — The San Antonio Spurs advanced to the Western Conference finals with a 139-109 dismantling of the Minnesota Timberwolves in Game 6 on Friday. items.headline: OG Anunoby Fully Participates In Practice
items.blurb: OG Anunoby Fully Participates In Practice — OG Anunoby was a full participant in the New York Knicks practice on Friday for the first time since injuring his hamstring in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference semifinals against the Philadelphia 76ers. items.headline: Duncan Robinson: Iffy for Game 7
items.blurb: Duncan Robinson: Iffy for Game 7 — Robinson is listed as questionable for Game 7 of the Eastern Conference Semifinals against the Cavaliers on Sunday due to lower-back soreness. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Top-10 Junior Isaiah Hill Commits To Purdue
items.blurb: Top-10 Junior Isaiah Hill Commits To Purdue — Junior center Isaiah Hill committed to Purdue on Friday, becoming the school's highest rated recruit since 2007. "[Head coach] Matt Painter keeps it real," Hill said. "He is a hard worker who coaches to win. Off the court he can be a funny guy, but you wouldn't know it. items.blurb: J.B. Bickerstaff Says Pistons 'Just Don't Quit' After Forcing Game 7 Versus Cavs — The Detroit Pistons defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers 115-94 in Game 6 on Friday night to force a Game 7 in the Eastern Conference semifinals. items.blurb: Why Steve Kerr backtracked on retirement decision to stay with Warriors — Golden State Warriors coach Steve Kerr will be back next season, but the story certainly could have been much different.

items.headline: Winners, losers from NBA playoffs: Spurs, Stephon Castle sink Timberwolves
items.blurb: Winners, losers from NBA playoffs: Spurs, Stephon Castle sink Timberwolves — On a Friday night with two huge Game 6 battles, the Detroit Pistons avoided elimination by out-shooting the Cleveland Cavaliers, while the San Antonio Spurs blew out the Minnesota Timberwolves to earn a date with the Oklahoma City Thunder in the Western Conference Finals.

items.blurb: Pistons' Cade Cunningham excited to return to Detroit for Game 7 vs. Cavaliers — The Detroit Pistons have officially forced a Game 7 against the Cleveland Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference semifinals after the team's Friday win, 115-94, on the road.

items.headline: Jalen Williams Ready For West Finals After Missing Six Games With Hamstring Strain
items.blurb: Jalen Williams Ready For West Finals After Missing Six Games With Hamstring Strain — Jalen Williams declared himself fully healthy for the Western Conference finals Friday, ending uncertainty over the Oklahoma City Thunder wing's availability after a six-game absence. Williams sustained a Grade 1 left hamstring strain during the second round against the Phoenix Suns. Williams addressed his status

items.headline: Warriors To Open Stephen Curry Extension Talks, Committed To Him Finishing Career With Franchise
items.blurb: Warriors To Open Stephen Curry Extension Talks, Committed To Him Finishing Career With Franchise — The Golden State Warriors plan to open contract extension discussions with Steph Curry this summer, general manager Mike Dunleavy confirmed Friday. Curry, 38, has one year remaining on his current deal worth more than $62 million and becomes extension-eligible in late August. Curry said last month he

items.headline: Warriors Expect Draymond Green To Return But Defer To His Decision
items.blurb: Warriors Expect Draymond Green To Return But Defer To His Decision — The Golden State Warriors want Draymond Green back, but the decision rests entirely with the veteran forward. General manager Mike Dunleavy made clear Friday that the organization has expressed its preference while leaving the choice to Green. items.blurb: Warriors Expected To Keep 11th Pick To Add Young Talent To Aging Roster — The Golden State Warriors front office is leaning toward keeping its 11th overall pick in an upcoming NBA draft widely considered strong through its lottery portion, sources told ESPN. The Warriors, carrying one of the oldest rosters in the league, are focused on adding younger talent to reinforce their depth. General manage

items.headline: Steve Kerr 'Thrilled' To Continue Coaching, Vows 'To Be Better' Next Season
items.blurb: Steve Kerr 'Thrilled' To Continue Coaching, Vows 'To Be Better' Next Season — Steve Kerr will coach the Golden State Warriors for a 13th season after a period of deliberation that included an honest personal evaluation. items.blurb: Bench players carry Pistons to Game 6 win over Cavaliers — Cade Cunningham has carried the Detroit Pistons all season. With that season on the verge of ending, the Pistons' bench came through in a big way in their 115-94 Game 6 win over the Cleveland Cavaliers to extend their time in the NBA playoffs.

items.headline: Pistons’ hustle sequence ends with Reed’s tough and-one vs. Cavaliers
items.blurb: Pistons’ hustle sequence ends with Reed’s tough and-one vs. Cavaliers — After a missed three nearly sailed out of bounds, the Detroit Pistons somehow kept the possession alive before Ausar Thompson found Paul Reed for a strong and-one finish to cap an incredible hustle sequence.

items.headline: Pistons’ Thompson skies for incredible two-handed block of Strus attempt
items.blurb: Pistons’ Thompson skies for incredible two-handed block of Strus attempt — Detroit Pistons star defender Ausar Thompson tracks the Cleveland Cavaliers’ Max Strus in transition and elevates for an unbelievable two-handed block during the third quarter of Game 6.

items.blurb: Cavaliers’ Harden enters top 10 in all-time playoff scoring with three — Watch as Cleveland Cavaliers guard James Harden hits his signature step-back three-pointer to officially move into the top 10 on the NBA’s all-time playoff scoring list.

items.headline: Steve Kerr remains committed to Warriors following doubts after season
items.blurb: Steve Kerr remains committed to Warriors following doubts after season — Once Steve Kerr came to the realization that there was nothing he’d rather do than coach the Golden State Warriors and the team figured out there was no one better to guide the organization through Stephen Curry’s final seasons, the final decision for both sides was easy.
